factors.name: Mega-cap tech sector rotation
factors.summary: Wall Street saw 'AI-linked stocks slide' on Thursday with chip stocks suffering 'worst day in more than two weeks.' This is a rotation OUT of Nasdaq-100 heavyweights (NVDA, semiconductors) into Financials/Industrials (Dow +1.73%, S&P higher, but NQ down on the day).

factors.name: Semiconductor weakness
factors.summary: Chipmakers saw their worst day in >2 weeks; director Mark Stevens (NVIDIA) sold $221.1M in stock. S&P rejected fast-track index rules for SpaceX, removing a near-term mega-cap IPO catalyst. No bullish semiconductor catalysts in pipeline.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk premium fading
factors.summary: Trump stated reluctance to restart Iran war, citing only renewed action if US troops killed. Oil down 3%, Iran ceasefire optimism rising, reducing macro risk premium that had supported growth/tech. This typically derisks equities and supports rotation into value.

factors.name: Real yields and inflation uncertainty
factors.summary: Fed's Schmid cited inflation as 'biggest risk' facing the economy; Treasury Sec Bessent noted disappointing job market. No major inflation data (CPI/PCE/NFP) released yet in this window; major NFP report due TODAY (Friday). Uncertainty high—real yields likely to move sharply post-data.

factors.name: Bitcoin and risk-on correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin down 13% this week (worst since February) with 13-day consecutive ETF outflows (record streak). Bitcoin trading below $60K 'fire sale' territory. This signals de-risking and liquidity rotation OUT of high-beta tech, which heavily correlates to NQ.

factors.name: Goldman Sachs overweight equities, but risk appetite at 4-year high
factors.summary: GS maintains 12-month overweight and recommends buying dips, BUT explicitly warns that its risk appetite indicator is at highest level since 2021, raising 'probability of near-term corrections.' This is a caution flag before major data releases.

factors.name: Quantinuum IPO disappointment; Honeywell Quantinuum closes flat
factors.summary: Quantinuum IPO opened 13% above price, closed flat. Lululemon cut full-year guidance on 'negative media commentary.' Insider buying (Insperity +4.9%, OfferPad CEO) present but sporadic—no clear conviction bottom.

factors.name: Lululemon guidance cut and retail weakness
factors.summary: Lululemon trimmed full-year forecast citing persistent US sales slump and product launch disappointments. Consumer discretionary weakness signals potential broader demand softness, pressuring growth multiples.

factors.name: AI narrative resilience but regulation questions
factors.summary: Anthropic warns AI labs need coordinated halt-development plans if risks rise; Google DeepMind CEO says AGI coming fast. SpaceX valued at $1.8T (100x AI revenue growth by 2030) but S&P rules block fast index entry. Regulation and execution risks remain high.

factors.name: US Dollar strength on Fed hawkishness
factors.summary: USD reverses earlier declines; DXY climbed to 99.45 on Fed hawkish commentary. Stronger USD pressures international earnings for Nasdaq-100 components and can tighten financial conditions, headwind for duration/growth.

factors.name: 4h and 24h price action
factors.summary: NAS100 down 0.15% over 4h, up 0.42% over 24h. Moves are small and choppy, consistent with Phase 2 consolidation before NFP. No strong directional conviction visible; likely range-bound into data.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a sector rotation away from mega-cap tech (especially semiconductors and AI-linked names) into Financials and value, as geopolitical risk premium fades with Iran ceasefire optimism. Bitcoin's sharp selloff and record ETF outflows signal broad de-risking and liquidity rotation, a headwind for high-beta growth. Real-yield and inflation uncertainty is elevated ahead of today's US NFP report, which could re-price Fed expectations sharply in either direction. Goldman Sachs remains overweight equities but warns risk appetite is at a 4-year high, suggesting downside tail risk into data. The index is consolidating in a tight range with no conviction directional bias.
catalysts_ahead: US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report – TODAY (Friday June 5, ~13:30 UTC). This is the single highest-impact event. Hot jobs data could push Fed hawkish and compress growth multiples; weak data could offer relief.
Fed commentary (Schmid flagged inflation as top risk; no FOMC meeting imminent but speakers active)
Semiconductor earnings season continuing; watch for capex guidance from NVDA, AMD, ASML
S&P 500 index changes announcement (likely today or Monday) – Marvell widely expected to join; could affect sector rotation flows
Iran ceasefire progress (ongoing negotiations; Trump-Khamenei meeting potential remains catalyst for oil/USD moves)
Private credit redemption stress update (Blackstone, Partners Group capped funds this week)
screening.risk_warning: If NFP comes in much hotter than expected (>250K jobs) or inflation surprises to the upside, real yields will spike and growth-heavy Nasdaq-100 will face sharp compression. Conversely, if NFP disappoints sharply and Fed-cut odds rise, the index could reverse higher. Geopolitical escalation in Iran or Ukraine could flip the risk-off narrative back to flight-to-safety in duration/bonds and away from equities. Continued Bitcoin weakness and crypto ETF outflows could signal sustained de-risking, which would keep near-term pressure on NQ. Marvell S&P 500 entry and potential SpaceX IPO chatter could also shift momentum into mega-cap growth names intraday.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is choppy and in Phase 2 consolidation ahead of major NFP data release. Avoid chasing short-term directional bets; wait for NFP print (TODAY) and retest of support (30,250) or resistance (30,550) before committing to a fresh position. If index breaks below 30,250 post-NFP, consider short entry. If it holds and bounces hard off support with confirmed reversal signal, consider long entry above 30,450.

factors.name: Semiconductor & AI Narrative Crack
factors.summary: Broadcom's AI-chip sales guidance disappointed, triggering broad semiconductor rotation. South Korea's KOSPI collapsed 6%, with Samsung and SK Hynix heavily sold. This is a key reversal of the AI-driven rally that has carried NDX higher YTD.

factors.name: Tech-Heavy Asia Capitulation
factors.summary: South Korean won hit 17-year lows (USD/KRW 1549), KOSPI down 6%, and foreign investors sold $10B in Korean equities in one week per Bloomberg. Regional tech selloff suggests NQ weakness could persist as Asia is a leading indicator for US markets.

factors.name: US NFP Data Imminent (May)
factors.summary: Critical employment report due Friday 12:30 GMT. Economists expect 'broadly stable' job growth, but miss could reignite Fed-cut bets (bullish equities) or beat could delay cuts (bearish). High uncertainty means volatility likely, capping confidence.

factors.name: Oil Supply Disruption & Geopolitical Tension
factors.summary: Drone attack halted Oman's Mina al Fahal crude loading. Iran threatens US bases and Strait of Hormuz. While Trump claims Iran deal 'very close,' repeated false starts have market skeptical (per Investinglive 'boy who cried wolf' commentary). Risk premium rising, demand destruction risk.

factors.name: Crypto Capitulation
factors.summary: Bitcoin treasury firms shed $62B in deepening crypto rout. NQ-BTC linkage remains tight; crypto weakness historically precedes risk-off in growth/tech equities. This is a secondary but material risk-off signal.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Pressure
factors.summary: While explicit yield data not in this batch, inflation remains sticky (RBI raised India inflation forecast to 5.1%, Indonesia expected 50bp more hikes). Long-duration Nasdaq components (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL) extremely sensitive to real rate re-pricing upward.

factors.name: Earnings Revision Risk
factors.summary: Lululemon cut FY guidance on 'negative media and weak product launches.' SpaceX blocked from S&P 500 fast-track entry despite mega-IPO hype. Earnings surprise risk tilting negative; mega-cap tech earnings season implications unclear but forward guidance cautious.

factors.name: 4h Price Action: Flat, 24h Mildly Positive
factors.summary: NAS100 -0.15% in 4h, +0.42% in 24h. Move is small, suggesting consolidation or exhaustion after recent highs. Nasdaq futures already showing bearish sentiment below VWAP per Investinglive (failed 30300 repair).

factors.name: Goldman Sachs Risk Appetite Warning
factors.summary: Goldman's risk appetite indicator at 4-year high raises probability of near-term correction, even as GS maintains overweight. Extreme readings historically precede volatility spikes and mean-reversion pullbacks.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Hype Deflated
factors.summary: S&P 500 rejected early-entry rules for SpaceX mega-IPO, killing narrative momentum. Mega-cap IPO arms race (Anthropic, OpenAI) also faces path-of-least-resistance resistance. Tech excitement moderating.

key_levels.support: 30,150–30,200 (recent pivot, Investinglive mentions 30218–30244 as bearish control band)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is at an inflection point after the AI-driven rally that began in late 2024. Broadcom's disappointing forward guidance cracked the semiconductor narrative, triggering a broad rotational selloff in mega-cap chip and tech names. South Korea's 6% KOSPI collapse and 17-year currency lows signal contagion risk across Asia-Pacific equities. While Trump's Iran deal rhetoric has provided periodic relief (oil stable ~$91), repeated false starts erode credibility, and geopolitical risk (Oman drone attack) re-emerged as a tail risk. Real yields remain sticky despite recent pivot-and-hope narrative; long-duration tech multiples face headwinds. Goldman's warning on peak risk appetite, Lululemon's negative guidance, and crypto capitulation all suggest a near-term correction is probable.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (May) – Friday 12:30 GMT – likely to be the single biggest mover; miss = bullish cuts, beat = hawkish hold
FOMC meeting (June 17–18 assumed) – forward guidance on rate path critical for 10-year yield and tech multiples
NVIDIA earnings and AI demand commentary – typically moves NQ 2–3%
MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings/guidance – each can move index 1–2%
China-Taiwan tensions escalation or chip export control news – could trigger broad tech selloff
PCE/Core CPI release – inflation data will drive Fed re-pricing
Treasury yield moves (10-year) – real-yield moves directly impact long-duration NDX components
Further cryptocurrency weakness or stabilization – sentiment indicator for risk-on/risk-off
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidation: (1) NFP beats significantly, pushing Fed-hold narrative → equities rally, NQ reverses bullish; (2) US-Iran deal clinched, oil collapses → geopolitical premium evaporates, risk-on unwind reverses; (3) New NVIDIA bullish guidance or hyperscaler capex acceleration → AI narrative reclaimed, mega-cap rerating higher; (4) 10-year Treasury yields drop sharply (real yields fall) → duration play re-accelerates NQ higher; (5) VIX stays subdued despite weakness → weak hands not capitulating, no selling climax, move could fizzle into chop rather than sustained correction.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (phase 2); avoid chasing the selloff. Wait for retest of 30,350–30,400 resistance or stabilization at 30,150–30,200 support with bullish divergence in VXN before considering fresh longs. Short entries on rallies into 30,500+ resistance; cover into NFP if data is soft.

factors.name: AI Trade Unwinding
factors.summary: Bitcoin plunged to near $62k, HYPE fell 14%, and South Korean tech stocks fell >5% as investor enthusiasm for AI stocks waned. This signals a broader risk-off rotation away from high-multiple growth/AI darlings that dominate NDX.

factors.name: Semiconductor Weakness
factors.summary: Broadcom issued disappointing AI guidance, chipmakers lagged despite S&P 500 breadth improving. Heavy NDX weighting in semis (NVDA, AVGO, AMD, QCOM) creates downside vulnerability.

factors.name: Futures Pre-NFP Caution
factors.summary: NQ JUN26 futures plunged 1.23% to 30,110 during European hours ahead of US NFP; Nasdaq analysis notes bearish bias below 30244 repair level with swing-bear targets at 30016–30000 if support fails.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk – Iran/Hormuz
factors.summary: Drone attack on Oman's Mina al Fahal crude terminal halts loading; US-Iran stalemate persists despite Trump's 'final stages deal' rhetoric. Sustained Middle East risk is depressing risk appetite and energy markets.

factors.name: Eurozone Contraction
factors.summary: Eurozone Q1 GDP revised down to -0.2% q/q (vs +0.1% second estimate), marking contraction at start of year with technical recession looming. Weak global growth environment pressures US growth expectations and multiple expansion.

factors.name: Valuation Frothiness Warning
factors.summary: Citigroup notes global stock markets at frothiest level since the global financial crisis, though Citi advises caution before panic. Heavy concentration in mega-cap tech leaves NDX exposed to multiple compression if growth disappoints.

factors.name: NFP Event Risk Imminent
factors.summary: US Nonfarm Payrolls due today at 12:30 GMT; markets are digesting UBS commentary that weak April Canadian data and sub-100k US payroll expectations could trigger rate-cut pricing or further volatility.

factors.name: Downside Momentum in Futures
factors.summary: Nasdaq futures failed repair attempt at 30244 and remain under pressure; technical bias is bearish with next swing-bear target near 30000. Prior Haiku bias was already SELL6/10 as of 05:16.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD trades lower around 99.30 ahead of NFP; Swiss Franc and commodity currencies showing modest strength. Weaker dollar slightly supportive for USD-revenue tech, but overshadowed by risk-off tone.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness
factors.summary: Bitcoin at risk of breaking below $60k per CoinDesk; Zcash plunged 30% on critical bug disclosure. Crypto selloff often signals broader risk-off and correlates with NDX weakness on unwind days.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is facing a sharp reversal of the AI trade as mega-cap tech enthusiasm wanes globally. Bitcoin and semis are selling off, Broadcom issued weak guidance, and South Korea's AI-heavy indices cratered >5%. Futures plunged 1.23% overnight as traders price risk-off ahead of a critical US NFP print today; technical analysis shows NQ broke below the 30244 repair level and is now testing 30000 as a swing-bear target. Geopolitical tension (Iran drone attack on Oman) and weak Eurozone growth (Q1 contraction) reinforce a risk-off, growth-pessimistic backdrop, though Citi warns valuations are already frothy.
catalysts_ahead: US Nonfarm Payrolls May (12:30 GMT today) — sub-100k or miss could trigger rate-cut repricing and additional equity weakness
Canadian Labour Force Survey (12:30 GMT today) — weak April data; upside surprise could support USD/CAD and weaken growth narrative
FOMC/Fed speakers early next week — market repricing based on NFP outcome
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings season (June–July) — forward guidance on cloud capex and AI demand critical
US inflation data (CPI, PCE) next week — could reset rate expectations and multiple assumptions
screening.risk_warning: If US NFP beats expectations (>200k jobs, sub-4% unemployment confirmed), or if Trump announces a binding Iran deal, risk-on sentiment could snap back quickly and short-squeeze NQ back above 30244. Conversely, if the move below 30000 accelerates and breaks key support, downside could extend toward 29500–29000. Watch dealer gamma and options expiry flows (multiple FX and equity expirations today) for sudden reversals.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (futures already down 1.23% overnight); consider entry on retest of 30244 resistance with tight stops above, or wait for pullback to 30100–30150 within the downtrend to build short positions. Avoid chasing after major NFP release until volatility settles; use NFP surprise direction to confirm next leg.

factors.name: AI Trade Faltering / Sector Rotation
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'S&P 500's Record Win Streak in Danger as AI Selloff Continues' with S&P 500 futures down 0.5% as of market open. Semiconductor heavyweights (Broadcom -12.6%, Micron -7.7%) sold off sharply yesterday; Nasdaq-100 is heavily exposed to mega-cap tech. Rotation out of AI into healthcare (XLV seeing strong call buying) is structural headwind for NQ.

factors.name: US May Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) Beat — Hawkish Surprise
factors.summary: NFP +172K vs +85K expected (Bloomberg, 12:35 GMT) — a major beat. Prior month revised UP to +179K. This strong labor data reduces near-term Fed cut odds (currently priced at only 16 bps by year-end per Investinglive 11:07). Stronger payrolls = higher real yields = compression of long-duration growth multiples that Nasdaq-100 thrives on.

factors.name: Real Yields / Long-Duration Growth Multiple Pressure
factors.summary: Hot NFP print typically re-prices Treasury yields higher and extends real yield curve steepness. Nasdaq-100 (80%+ mega-cap growth/AI) is extremely sensitive to real rate increases. With Fed on hold and inflation sticky, real yields likely to grind higher into summer.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength (DXY Implications)
factors.summary: Despite some near-term USD weakness before NFP, strong jobs data and structural USD support (FXStreet notes 'Fed hike odds remain, strong US data supports dollar') should re-anchor USD higher post-NFP. Stronger USD headwind for tech revenue (Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple have heavy international exposure).

factors.name: Volatility / Tech Sector Positioning
factors.summary: VXN (Nasdaq vol) implied to be elevated; dealer gamma in NVDA/AAPL likely compressed after multi-week rally. Premarket movers show weakness (Lululemon, DocuSign down). Options market pricing shows rotation risk into month-end rebalancing (BNY notes 'Rotation risks build into June rebalancing').

factors.name: Geopolitical / Macro Uncertainty (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Ongoing US-Iran stalemate with 'little progress' (Bloomberg 12:04 GMT) adds tail risk to oil, equities risk premium. Trump comments on 'final stages' deal ring hollow; markets pricing in extended uncertainty. Backdrop of geopolitical tension typically favors de-risking in growth names.

factors.name: Crypto Sector Weakness (Risk-Off Indicator)
factors.summary: Bitcoin 'caps dismal week 50% below ATH' (Bloomberg 12:31); ZEC crashes 38% on critical vulnerability. Crypto weakness is historically a leading indicator of equity risk-off. NQ-BTC correlation elevated; weak crypto = weak appetite for growth/speculative names.

factors.name: Prior Bias History Consistency
factors.summary: Your last two reads (06-05 05:16 'SELL 6/10' and 06-05 09:44 'SELL 5/10') suggest sustained bearish lean. NFP beat + rotation underway = continuation of that thesis, though no 'confirmation triggered' yet — move is still unfolding (PHASE_2).

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 is caught in a classic post-AI-boom rotation: the record winning streak in tech has stalled as investors front-run geopolitical risk and take profits in mega-cap growth. The May NFP print of +172K (crushing the +85K consensus) is a hawkish surprise that re-prices Fed hold expectations higher and pressures long-duration growth valuations—exactly where Nasdaq-100 concentrates. Semiconductor leaders (NVDA-adjacent supply chain) have already sold off 5-12% this week. Real yields are grinding higher, a structural headwind for the entire NQ complex. Concurrent weakness in crypto (BTC, ZEC) signals broader risk-off sentiment. The move is underway but not yet extended; this is a Phase 2 sell, not a capitulation.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision (11 June) — Nomura expects hike; could trigger EUR appreciation and USD weakness, brief relief for NQ
Any further mega-cap earnings revisions (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) given capex headwinds (Alphabet chasing $190B capex)
FOMC meeting (17-18 June) — Fed clarity on cut timing; currently markets see no cuts in 2026 near-term
CPI print (next week) — inflation surprise (up or down) will dominate bond/equity repricing
Semiconductor order/guidance updates (NVIDIA, TSMC, ASML) — AI demand trajectory remains the longest-duration story
US-Iran ceasefire news — geopolitical de-escalation could boost risk-on, reverse some of this week's losses
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran tensions ease suddenly or if a 'dovish surprise' from ECB/BoE next week triggers a risk-on reversal, the AI trade could reignite and Nasdaq-100 could snap back 1-2% within hours. Additionally, if CPI comes in cooler than expected (below 3%), that re-opens the door to Fed cuts in H2 2026, which would be massively bullish for long-duration growth. Conversely, if NFP weakness emerges in June, that accelerates the dovish repricing. Finally, Nvidia earnings/forward guidance (dates TBD) remain the single largest NDX catalyst — any weakness there could trigger 3-4% downside on the index.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (underway post-NFP surprise). Avoid chasing shorts here. Better entry on a retest/bounce to 30,500-30,600 resistance, or wait for a 2-3% pullback to mean-revert before considering fresh shorts. If NDX breaks 30,150 support decisively on closing basis, that opens downside to 29,800-30,000.

factors.name: May NFP Beat & Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: US Nonfarm Payrolls rose 172K vs 85K expected (Bloomberg, MarketWatch 13:55), with 93K upward revisions. Bond traders now fully price a Fed rate hike by end-2026 (Bloomberg 12:47), crushing long-duration growth multiples.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Spike
factors.summary: 10-year yields surging on hot jobs data; bond market repricing hawkish (Bloomberg Markets 15:49, Bloomberg 12:47). Higher real yields compress Nasdaq valuations significantly.

factors.name: Tech Sector Meltdown
factors.summary: Nasdaq slides 2% on tech sell-off (Stock Market News 15:32); NVDA down 3.25%, AMD down 6.27% (Investinglive 14:46). Semiconductor sector under heavy pressure.

factors.name: Nasdaq Index Performance
factors.summary: Nasdaq Composite down 1.9%, Nasdaq below moving averages (Investinglive 14:07, 14:01). Tech specifically leading the decline vs broader S&P 500 (-1.1%).

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: Dollar rallies sharply post-NFP (FXStreet 15:02, EUR/USD to two-month lows at 1.1559). Strong dollar headwind for Nasdaq components with international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Combination of higher nominal yields and inflation persistence (Fed's Hammack notes persistently high inflation, 14:29) drives real yields higher, undermining tech growth multiples.

factors.name: VIX/Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: Equity rout in progress; commodities (gold -2%, silver -6.74%) selling off (FXStreet 15:02, 15:00) consistent with risk-off unwind from crowded long positioning.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Commentary
factors.summary: Fed's Hammack reaffirms inflation concerns and suggests 'holding steady may not be enough' (Investinglive 14:29). Market pricing now assumes higher-for-longer rates.

factors.name: Prior Sell Bias Continuation
factors.summary: Three prior Haiku reads (05:16, 09:44, 12:36) all signaled SELL 5-6/10 without confirmation. Current NFP surprise provides the hawkish catalyst to trigger follow-through selling.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness (Risk-Off Proxy)
factors.summary: Bitcoin breaks $60K support (down 4.5% to $60.7K, Investinglive 14:15); XRP at multi-month lows. Crypto weakness signals broader risk-off, NQ-BTC correlation negative today.

screening.narrative: May's blowout NFP (172K vs 85K, +93K revisions) has triggered a sharp repricing of Fed rate-hike odds, with bond markets now fully pricing a 2026 hike. Real yields are rising sharply, crushing Nasdaq valuations—long-duration growth multiples are particularly vulnerable. Tech is bleeding hard (Nasdaq -1.9%, semiconductor heavyweights like NVDA -3.25%, AMD -6.27%), and the move is accelerating into the close as institutional flows rotate out of crowded growth positions. This is no longer a bounce-and-reverse scenario; it is a forced liquidation of overextended tech longs.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway and accelerating; do not chase shorts at open Monday. Better entries likely on any bounce/retest of 19,700–19,750 resistance, or on breakdown confirmation through 19,200 support. Watch for Monday morning reversal or continued sellers into the week.
catalysts_ahead: Monday US equity open – potential gap down or consolidation
CPI/Core CPI next week – market extremely sensitive to inflation surprises given hawkish repricing
FOMC minutes mid-week – further rate-hike guidance could extend selloff or stabilize
Tech earnings week of June 9–13 (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN macro commentary on capex & guidance)
Fed speaker calendar – watch for hawkish vs dovish tone over coming days
screening.risk_warning: If Monday opens and sells off further on gap-down opening, shorts may face consolidation or short-squeeze if VXN spikes >25 and retail panic-buying triggers a relief rally. Conversely, if Treasuries stabilize (10Y yields hold 4.2–4.3%) and earnings beat expectations next week, the bear case could unwind quickly. Invalidation occurs if Nasdaq reclaims 19,800 on conviction intraday with tech rotation reversing.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move in progress; wait for intraday bounce toward 20,000-20,100 resistance to add short exposure, or chase current levels with tight stops if conviction high on Fed hike cycle.

factors.name: NFP Blowout & Fed Re-pricing
factors.summary: May NFP crushed expectations at 172K vs 85K est., with prior months revised +93K higher. Unemployment held 4.3%, wages +3.4% YoY. Markets now fully pricing Fed hike by year-end per Bloomberg; hawkish inflection is severe for long-duration tech.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports stocks and bonds falling as jobs fuel Fed-hike bets; bond traders fully priced in 2026 rate hike, spurring yields higher across $31T Treasury market. Rising nominal yields compress NDX multiples directly.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Hot labor data + Fed hike pricing = higher real yields (TIPS). Long-duration growth stocks (NDX core) are exceptionally sensitive to real rate moves; this is a structural headwind for tech multiples.

factors.name: Tech Sector Carnage
factors.summary: Per Investinglive: 'Tech meltdown deepens' with NVDA -3.25%, AMD -6.27%. Nasdaq slides 2% on tech sell-off per Stock Market News. Semiconductors leading the decline confirms sector-wide risk-off rotation.

factors.name: Fed Officials Turn Hawkish
factors.summary: Fed's Hammack stated holding rates steady 'remains reasonable' but warned policymakers may soon need to act on persistently high inflation. Jobs report 'reaffirms jobs market is roughly in balance,' giving Fed room to tighten.

factors.name: Risk-Off Across Assets
factors.summary: Bitcoin plunged to $60K (-4.5%), XRP toward $1.10, crypto 'dismal week' per MarketWatch. Gold down >2%, Silver -6.74%. Broad risk-off confirms flight from high-beta growth assets like NDX.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: Bloomberg: 'Dollar rises as jobs data boosts Fed rate-hike expectations.' NDX components derive ~40-50% revenue internationally; stronger USD = FX headwind to earnings and multiple compression for mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Credit Market Caution
factors.summary: GoldenTree's Tananbaum spots 'credit-equity disconnect' with opportunities in sectors facing business-model challenges including software. Credit tightening and equity volatility often precede tech corrections.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility Regime
factors.summary: MarketWatch cites 'wild single-stock price swings' and sector divergence (tech melting down, consumer defensive resilient). Elevated single-name vol and sector dispersion typical of corrective phases.

factors.name: AI Bubble Debate Intensifying
factors.summary: Despite BI's Schiffman claiming 'there is no AI bubble,' MarketWatch warns 'two classic signs of a market top are looming' citing capex recycling concerns. Sentiment shift from euphoria to skepticism is a contrarian red flag for NDX longs.

factors.name: Macro Backdrop: Iran Tensions
factors.summary: US-Iran peace deal talks stalled, SPR to refill +40M barrels post-conflict. Energy volatility and geopolitical risk typically modest direct impact on NDX, but adds to macro uncertainty backdrop.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Overhang
factors.summary: SpaceX $75B IPO oversubscribed, targeting retail investors per Bloomberg. Large IPO can drain liquidity from secondary tech names short-term, but not a primary NDX driver.

confirmation.narrative: NFP's massive beat (172K vs 85K) and upward revisions have triggered full repricing of Fed trajectory, with markets now pricing a rate hike by year-end. This is a regime shift: Treasury yields surging, real yields climbing, and long-duration tech multiples compressing violently. NDX down ~2% intraday with semis leading losses (NVDA -3.25%, AMD -6.27%). Broader risk-off confirmed by crypto collapse (BTC -4.5% to $60K), metals selloff, and USD strength. Fed's Hammack reinforced hawkish tilt, citing balanced labor market gives room to act on inflation. Credit markets flashing caution signals and AI euphoria showing cracks. This is not a headline fake-out—fundamental repricing of growth discount rates is underway.
catalysts_ahead: ECB decision June (likely hike per Nordea/TD Sec, may influence Fed expectations)
Next US CPI print (critical for Fed trajectory confirmation)
NVDA/mega-cap tech earnings (any capex or demand guidance shifts)
Fed speakers through next week (Hammack's hawkish tone likely echoed)
10Y yield breaking key technical levels (watch 4.5%+ for acceleration)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Fed speakers aggressively push back on hike expectations in next 48h, (2) 10Y yields reverse sharply lower (e.g., flight-to-quality on new crisis), (3) mega-cap tech announces unexpectedly strong guidance or major buybacks, (4) NFP data revised significantly lower in coming weeks. Current move is Phase 2 (in progress ~2h post-NFP); chasing here carries execution risk—prefer waiting for bounce to resistance.

factors.name: May NFP Blowout & Fed Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: US Nonfarm Payrolls 'smashed forecasts' (per multiple sources) with 3-month upward revisions, crushing market expectations for Fed rate cuts. Prediction markets now price 52% odds of a Fed HIKE this year (vs. prior cut expectations), driving nominal yields higher and compressing long-duration growth multiples that Nasdaq 100 heavily depends on.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yields & Real Rates Rising
factors.summary: Strong NFP triggered immediate Treasury yield rally; gold down 3.2% ($143 fall) and below 200-hour MA, signaling broad risk-off and higher real rates (TIPS correlation). Mega-cap tech with long-duration cash flows are extremely sensitive to real rate moves—direct headwind for NAS100.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY) Breaking to 100+
factors.summary: DXY rallied above 100 on NFP surprise; stronger dollar erodes international revenue for Nasdaq 100 constituents (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, NVDA all have 40-50%+ overseas exposure). Currency headwind will dampen earnings guidance.

factors.name: Tech Sector Sell-Off In Progress
factors.summary: Nasdaq fell 'nearly 3%' on tech sell-off (per news); semiconductor heavyweights NVDA down 3.25%, AMD down 6.27%. Retail investors piled into semis in May and are now reversing, triggering cascade liquidations. This confirms PHASE_2 momentum lower.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation Breakdown (BTC below $60K)
factors.summary: Bitcoin crashed to lowest since October 2024 ($59,743 low), breaking key psychological $60K. BTC-NQ correlation typically positive in risk-on; BTC weakness signals broader risk-off sentiment and likely further equity volatility into Monday.

factors.name: Inflation Narrative Shift
factors.summary: Hot jobs data combined with persistent wage pressure (Fed Hammack: 'inflation remains above 2% target') suggests 'higher for longer' rates thesis is winning. This kills the narrative that support equity multiples—growth stocks priced for Fed easing are now vulnerable to sustained restrictive policy.

factors.name: Retail Investor Positioning Reversal
factors.summary: MarketWatch notes retail investors were heaviest buyers of semiconductors in May; now they're likely cutting positions as momentum turns negative. Potential for cascading stop-loss selling if NAS100 breaks key technical support.

factors.name: AI Capex Narrative Under Pressure
factors.summary: News reports model routing and cost optimization in AI spending (OpenAI, Anthropic cutting costs); this could slow capex acceleration expectations. However, no mega-cap earnings guidance yet, so impact remains muted.

factors.name: Geopolitical (Iran-US, Taiwan, Trump Policy)
factors.summary: Iran-US talks stalled, Strait of Hormuz traffic minimal, but oil down $2.30 (modest impact). Trump policy churn (DOGE whistleblowers, MSTR Bitcoin sales, SpaceX IPO focus) adds noise but no direct market-moving catalyst yet.

factors.name: Earnings Season & Guidance Risk
factors.summary: Apple WWDC (Tim Cook AI legacy 'at stake'), Microsoft cloud capex commentary, and NVDA AI demand guidance all pending. If hyperscalers guide lower on capex or AI demand, expect sharp 3-5% NAS100 selloff. Current setup is vulnerable to negative surprises.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in PHASE 2 of a downtrend triggered by Friday's stronger-than-expected May NFP (jobs beat forecasts, 3-month revisions up), which has forced a violent repricing of Fed rate expectations from 'cuts in 2H 2026' to '52% odds of a hike this year.' This kills the 'lower for longer' narrative that supported equity multiples, especially long-duration growth stocks (tech, semis). The 10Y Treasury yield is rallying, real yields are rising, the USD is breaking above 100, and Nasdaq has already fallen ~3% with semiconductors leading losses (NVDA -3.25%, AMD -6.27%). Bitcoin below $60K confirms risk-off mood. The move is underway—we're not at the start anymore—but downside momentum is strong and retail positioning in semis (heavy May buyers) is likely reversing into stops.
catalysts_ahead: Apple WWDC (next few days) — AI strategy & services guidance
NVIDIA, Microsoft, Google, Amazon earnings prep (likely next 2-3 weeks) — cloud capex and AI demand commentary
ECB June MPC decision (mid-month) — any hawkish shift will reinforce higher-for-longer narrative
Next CPI/PCE release — any surprise higher will accelerate Fed hike repricing
FOMC meeting minutes or Powell commentary — signal whether Fed is considering hikes
US Treasury auction volatility and yield curve reaction
screening.risk_warning: If jobs data is revised down next week, or if CPI/PCE surprisingly cools, or if a mega-cap reports monster guidance (capex acceleration, AI demand surge), the 'Fed cuts back on table' narrative can snap back, triggering sharp 2-3% NAS100 rally and short squeeze. Also watch for any dovish Fed speaker (Powell, Barkin) who could talk down rate hike odds. Supply/demand in QQQ ETF and single-name dealer gamma in NVDA/AAPL/MSFT could amplify whipsaws.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is PHASE 2 (in progress, ~3h into sell-off); wait for retest of 29,600–29,700 resistance or fresh support around 29,000 before considering short entries. If NAS100 breaks 28,800 with volume, expect fast move to 28,500. Do not chase shorts here—better risk/reward is waiting for pullback or entry on break of new lows with confirmation.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move underway but not fully extended; short rallies toward 29,600-29,800 resistance, or add on breakdown below 29,300 with tight stops.

factors.name: NFP blowout + Fed rate hike repricing
factors.summary: May NFP crushed estimates at a level well above the 85K consensus (Finance, MarketWatch, Bloomberg), pushing unemployment to 4.3% and triggering a hawkish Fed repricing. Prediction markets now see 52% chance of a Fed hike this year (CNBC). Higher-for-longer rates compress long-duration tech multiples severely.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury yield surge
factors.summary: Strong jobs data sent yields sharply higher (FXStreet, Bloomberg), compressing valuations for high-multiple Nasdaq growth stocks. Real yields also rising, a double headwind for long-duration equities.

factors.name: Nasdaq down nearly 3% on tech sell-off
factors.summary: All News wire reports Nasdaq fell nearly 3% on tech sell-off and hawkish signals from May jobs data. Price action confirms the selloff is in motion—down -1.25% in 24h, accelerating into -2.8% intraday.

factors.name: Semiconductor stocks under heavy pressure
factors.summary: NVDA down -3.25%, AMD down -6.27% (Investinglive). Semi sector is the primary NQ driver and is leading the decline. Sector weakness amplifies index downside given heavy weighting.

factors.name: Bitcoin breaking below $60K
factors.summary: BTC fell below $60,000 to lowest since October 2024 (CNBC, CoinDesk, Decrypt). Crypto-NQ correlation remains elevated on risk-on/risk-off days; crypto collapse signals broad risk-asset retreat and tech sentiment deterioration.

factors.name: US Dollar strength (DXY above 100)
factors.summary: DXY rallied above 100 on NFP beat (FXStreet), pressuring NDX mega-caps with heavy international revenue exposure (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA). EUR/USD at two-month lows, USD/CHF at two-month highs confirm broad dollar surge.

factors.name: VIX and risk sentiment spike
factors.summary: Gold plunging -3.2% and breaking 200-hour MA for first time since October 2023 (Investinglive) signals flight from risk assets. Tech-specific vol (VXN) likely spiking alongside broader market stress.

factors.name: Fed's Hammack reaffirms hawkish stance
factors.summary: Cleveland Fed President Hammack said holding rates steady remains reasonable but warned if trends continue, action may be needed to address persistently high inflation (FXStreet). Reinforces no near-term cuts, potential hike risk.

factors.name: Model routing threatening OpenAI/Anthropic margins
factors.summary: CNBC reports companies shifting to model routing vs. running everything on most powerful AI models—cost optimization threatening hyperscaler AI demand and revenue assumptions for MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN cloud businesses.

factors.name: Apple WWDC overhang (Tim Cook's AI legacy at stake)
factors.summary: CNBC notes AAPL's WWDC has Tim Cook's AI legacy, Siri's future, and rich valuation on the line. Event-driven uncertainty caps any reflexive bounce attempts but not yet a clear directional signal.

factors.name: Retail piled into semis in May—contrarian signal
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports May was strongest month for retail buying into semiconductor stocks. Heavy retail positioning at highs often marks sentiment extremes and precedes sharp reversals—retail as exit liquidity.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO oversubscribed
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports SpaceX $75B IPO already oversubscribed, setting record. Musk demanding retail ownership (MarketWatch). Large IPO can drain liquidity from secondary tech names short-term, but not a primary driver.

factors.name: Regulatory and geopolitical noise
factors.summary: Coinbase downgraded on regulatory uncertainty, US states preparing lawsuit to block Paramount-Warner Bros (All News), Illinois pausing data center tax breaks amid AI concerns. Incremental regulatory headwinds for tech sector breadth.

confirmation.narrative: The May NFP blowout crushed any lingering Fed dovish hopes, triggering a violent repricing toward potential rate hikes and sending 10-year yields surging. Nasdaq is down nearly 3% intraday as long-duration mega-cap tech—NVDA, AMD, AAPL—craters under the weight of higher real yields and a surging dollar. The move is Phase 2 (well underway but not fully exhausted), with semis leading the decline and crypto breaking multi-month lows confirming broad risk-asset deleveraging. Retail was max long semis in May—classic sentiment extreme now reversing violently.
catalysts_ahead: Apple WWDC (next week) - AI strategy, Siri agentic platform reveal
Next CPI print (mid-June) - will determine if Fed hike pricing sticks or fades
FOMC meeting (late June) - dot plot revisions, rate path guidance
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL earnings (next earnings cycle ~July) - hyperscaler capex guidance critical
Any US-Iran escalation headlines (Hormuz traffic near zero, talks deadlocked per CNN/Investinglive)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if upcoming CPI prints softer than expected, Fed officials push back hard on hike pricing, or a major mega-cap (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA) announces transformational AI product/partnership that reignites growth narrative. Technical invalidation: sustained reclaim and hold above 30,000 with conviction.

factors.name: NFP Catalyst — Hot Jobs Data
factors.summary: May NFP came in at 172K vs. 85K expected, with prior 3 months revised upward. This crushes Fed-cut expectations and has pushed prediction markets to 52% odds of a rate HIKE this year (per US Top News). Core Nasdaq underperformance (-3.84% intraday) confirms equity repricing.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: US yields 'skyrocket across the whole curve' on Friday per FXStreet; 10Y up ~6bps, 2Y up ~12bps. Long-duration growth tech (Nasdaq's core holding) extremely sensitive to rising real rates; yield surge = multiple compression for unprofitable/high-growth names.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: Nominal yield spike + lower inflation expectations = real yields rising. High-duration Nasdaq tech (AAPL, MSFT, TSLA, NVDA) trade on terminal-value models; rising real rates compress terminal multiples.

factors.name: AI Capex Rotation & Dilution Fears
factors.summary: Meta considering tens of billions in equity raise for AI (FT report), Microsoft possibly tapping equity markets for AI infrastructure. Google already did $85B sale. Equity dilution + capex intensity concerns pressuring mega-cap sentiment. NVDA options surged 605K contracts on hedging.

factors.name: Sector Repricing: Tech Selloff
factors.summary: Nasdaq down 3.84% intraday to 25,801 per Investinglive. MSFT, META, GOOGL leading declines. Chip rally careening to halt per headline. Lululemon cut guidance; single-name cascades underway.

factors.name: Fed Policy Shift — Rate Hikes Back on Table
factors.summary: Citigroup lone voice for cuts now contrarian. Chair Warsh faces tests; Trump left rate decision to him, but strong data extends hiking window (May NFP + revised priors). Fed pivot away from dovish scenario = headwind for equities.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY >100)
factors.summary: USD rallied to 100.10 on strong NFP, up from 99.16 low. Strong dollar pressures international revenues for Nasdaq names (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA all have 40%+ international exposure). Headwind for earnings translation.

factors.name: Crypto Cascade — BTC Below $60K
factors.summary: Bitcoin crashed below $60K to $59,743 (Oct 2024 lows), wiping $200B in crypto market cap. Zcash vulnerability, risk-off sentiment. Crypto flush = spillover to Nasdaq-correlated risk trades (TSLA, MSTR, growth names).

factors.name: Prior Bias Consistency
factors.summary: Haiku escalated from SELL6/10 → SELL7/10 → SELL9/10 on 2026-06-05. Sonnet tagged SELL8/10 → SELL9/10. Confluence of prior reads + fresh catalyst = sustained downside bias.

factors.name: Move Already Underway — Extended Phase
factors.summary: NAS100 down 2.29% in 24h, down 1.02% in 4h. Intraday low 25,753; current 29,089 suggests some bounce from session lows but still significantly lower. Move is not fresh; we are in retracement/consolidation phase, not early stages.

key_levels.support: 28,800–28,900 (session lows, 4h support zone); 28,500 (next key floor if breakdown); 27,500 (weekly support)
key_levels.resistance: 29,500–29,800 (intraday bounces, offers shorting); 30,200 (recent swing high pre-selloff)

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq100 is in a sharp repricing cycle following a blowout May NFP (172K vs. 85K expected), which has killed Fed-cut narratives and sent 10Y yields surging 6–12bps. Rising real yields and a strengthening dollar are brutal for long-duration tech growth stocks. Compounding this, mega-cap tech faces dilution fears from massive AI capex equity raises (Meta, Microsoft) and capex-intensity anxiety. Bitcoin's breakdown below $60K adds risk-off spillover. The move is already 2.3% in 24h; we are in Phase 3 (late/extended), not chasing lower, but the structural repricing (rates, multiples, foreign FX headwinds) remains intact. Any bounce should be sold into resistance.
catalysts_ahead: CPI / Core CPI data (next week) — if hot, accelerates rate-hike bets; if cool, relieves some yield pressure
Apple WWDC (next week) — Tim Cook's final developer conference; AI strategy and commentary critical for mega-cap sentiment
FOMC meeting (likely mid-June if not already passed) — Chair Warsh's first major policy decision under Trump; market pricing in June hike debate
NVDA earnings / AI demand commentary — key barometer for chip and hyperscaler capex outlook
SpaceX IPO (next week) — large equity raise, sentiment on venture/IPO calendar
Treasury auctions / 10Y yield stabilization — if yields continue higher, another leg lower in growth tech
screening.risk_warning: Oversold bounce: If Nasdaq mean-reverts 2–3% on light volume or profit-taking, short-term traders will cover. Watch for bounces to 29,500–30,000 resistance as sell targets. If CPI comes in cooler than expected or Fed signals dovish pivot next week, NAS100 will reverse hard northward (invalidating sell bias). Earnings season (META, MSFT, AAPL guidance beats) could also spark relief rallies. Watch VIX and dealer gamma in NVDA/AAPL for tail-risk capitulation signals.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended (Phase 3); avoid chasing lower. Wait for retest of 29,500–29,800 resistance zone for short re-entry. Alternatively, if 28,500–28,800 support breaks decisively on heavy volume, that signals capitulation, but risk/reward is poor this late. Consider puts / hedges for rallies into 30K resistance rather than fresh shorts here.

factors.name: NFP Jobs Report - Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: May NFP at 172K massively beat expectations of 85K, with upward revisions to prior months. Bloomberg reports Citi maintains lonely call for cuts while market now prices 52% chance of Fed hike this year per prediction markets. Strong labor market removes case for easing.

factors.name: 10-Year Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: FXStreet reports US Treasury yields 'rocketing' across curve; 2-year up 12+ bps, 10-year up 6+ bps on NFP beat. Higher nominal yields compress long-duration tech multiples significantly.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Equity Dilution Risk
factors.summary: Meta considering tens of billions in stock offering for AI push (FT/Bloomberg), following Google's record $85B raise this week. Meta down -6.56% to $586, contributing heavily to NDX decline. Dilution fears spreading across hyperscalers.

factors.name: Chip Rally Collapse
factors.summary: All News headline: 'Nasdaq craters 4% as chip rally careens to a halt.' Marvell Technology options surge to 605K contracts suggesting heavy hedging. Semiconductor weakness is core NDX drag.

factors.name: Microsoft AI Capex Funding Concerns
factors.summary: Investinglive reports Microsoft may tap equity markets to fund AI infrastructure, weighing on MSFT and broader tech sector. Accelerated selling pressure followed this news with NDX down -3.84% at session lows.

factors.name: Bitcoin/Crypto Risk-Off Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin broke below $60K to $59,743 (lowest since Oct 2024), down over 50% from October peak. Crypto weakness typically correlates with NDX risk-off sentiment; Strategy (MSTR) under pressure from shorts.

factors.name: VIX/Volatility Spike
factors.summary: Goldman's Flood calls Friday's selloff a 'buying opportunity' but notes 'slight disconnect' between retail and institutional investors, suggesting elevated volatility and positioning stress.

factors.name: Strong USD Post-NFP
factors.summary: DXY rallied to near 100.10 from 99.16 low on NFP beat. FXStreet: 'NFP steamrolls US Dollar bears.' Stronger dollar headwind for NDX multinationals with heavy international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Combination of nominal yield surge and inflation expectations repricing upward means real yields climbing—extremely negative for long-duration growth stocks that dominate NDX.

factors.name: Technical Breakdown
factors.summary: NDX down -2.29% in 24h, -1.02% in 4h, currently at 29,089 with session low at 25,753 reported (likely typo for different index but indicates severe selling). Move accelerating into close suggests momentum continuation.

factors.name: AI Regulation & Antitrust Overhang
factors.summary: House panel proposes new digital asset tax rules; Trump admin discussing OpenAI government stake; Anthropic NSA partnership raises AI militarization concerns. Regulatory uncertainty weighing on AI-driven rally thesis.

factors.name: Lululemon Guidance Cut
factors.summary: Lulu cut annual forecast amid slumping North America sales, only 1 analyst remains bullish. Consumer discretionary weakness signals broader economic concerns affecting NDX consumer names.

confirmation.narrative: The NDX is experiencing a violent repricing following a blowout NFP report that shattered dovish Fed expectations and sent Treasury yields surging. The 172K jobs print (vs 85K expected) has ignited 52% odds of a Fed *hike* this year, compressing tech multiples as 10-year yields spike 6+ bps. Simultaneously, mega-cap equity dilution fears (Meta's potential tens-of-billions raise after Google's record $85B) are triggering sector-wide selling, with the chip rally 'careening to a halt.' The combination of higher nominal/real yields, stronger dollar, crypto correlation breakdown (BTC sub-$60K), and AI capex funding concerns creates a perfect storm for long-duration growth. This is Phase 2 of the move—initial selling has pushed NDX down -2.29% in 24h with acceleration into the close, but momentum remains bearish into the weekend.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI data (upcoming week per Bloomberg)
Apple WWDC June 2026 - Tim Cook's final conference, AI legacy on the line
SpaceX IPO pricing next week ($23B retail component)
FOMC meeting (likely July or September based on Trump October comment)
House hearing on 7 new crypto tax bills (Tuesday)
Potential Microsoft/Meta equity offering announcements
confirmation.risk_warning: Invalidation would require: (1) Fed Chair Warsh explicitly pushing back on hike expectations, (2) significant softening in next week's CPI print restoring rate-cut narrative, (3) mega-cap earnings pre-announcements showing AI revenue acceleration justifying capex, or (4) technical reclaim of 29,500 resistance with volume. Goldman calling this a 'buying opportunity' suggests institutional dip-buying possible, but needs catalyst.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is underway but not fully extended; ideal short entry on any relief bounce to 29,300-29,500 resistance zone, or add to shorts on break below 28,800 support with tight stops above 29,100.

factors.name: NFP Data Shock (May Jobs Report)
factors.summary: US Nonfarm Payrolls came in at 172K vs 85K expected, well above consensus. This is the dominant catalyst driving a sharp repricing of Fed rate-hike expectations higher and pushing Treasury yields up across the curve (10Y +6bps, 2Y +12bps per reports), which compresses long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: With nominal yields spiking on stronger-than-expected growth and inflation signals contained, real yields are moving higher. Nasdaq-100 is ultra-sensitive to real rate moves (inverse correlation); rising real rates are a direct headwind for high-multiple, long-duration growth stocks.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Capex & Equity Dilution Fears
factors.summary: Google, Microsoft, and Meta are all signaling massive capex commitments and potential large equity offerings to fund AI infrastructure. Alphabet raised $85B in stock sales this week; Meta and Microsoft are considering tens of billions more. Equity dilution and capex concerns are weighing heavily on tech stocks.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Collapse
factors.summary: Marvell, Micron, and the broader chip sector suffered their worst day in 6 years Friday. Chip makers dominated S&P 500 losers. Semiconductor stocks are core NDX holdings and their weakness directly drags the index.

factors.name: USD Rally & Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY broke above 100.10 on the back of the strong NFP print. Stronger dollar headwinds Nasdaq-100 components with significant international revenue exposure (MSFT, GOOG, AAPL, etc.).

factors.name: Tech-Specific Selloff & Risk-Off Rotation
factors.summary: Nasdaq down 4.18% on the day (worst since April 2025), worse than S&P 500 (-2.65%) and Dow (-1.35%). Clear rotation out of high-multiple growth into value/defensive. Meta down 6.56% after raising equity capital concerns; Lululemon cut guidance and saw analyst downgrades.

factors.name: Fed Hike Bets Surging
factors.summary: Market now repricing in higher probability of Fed rate hikes (not cuts) following strong labor data. Citi's three-cut call is now a 'lonely' outlier. Warsh at Fed faces imminent policy pressure; Trump's public comments wanting rate cuts are being ignored by markets.

factors.name: Prior Analyst Bias Consistency
factors.summary: Your prior three reads (2026-06-05 15:54, 17:54, 19:53) all escalated from SELL 7/10 → SELL 8-9/10. This move has sustained and deepened, validating the bearish thesis across the full day.

factors.name: Move Already Extended
factors.summary: 4h change: -1.47%, 24h change: -2.73%. NAS100 down >3% intraday (was -3.84% at session lows near 25,753). This is a Phase 3, extended move — chasing shorts here is late-stage and risky.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness (Risk-Off Proxy)
factors.summary: Bitcoin crashed below $60K (lowest since October 2024) on the strong jobs report and rising yields. Crypto weakness is a risk-off signal that correlates with tech selloffs; MSTR and crypto-linked equities tanking as well.

key_levels.resistance: 29200–29400 (intraday high before selloff); 29800 (prior day close area)

screening.narrative: A blowout May NFP report (172K jobs vs 85K expected) triggered a violent repricing of Fed rate expectations, sending Treasury yields sharply higher and compressing long-duration tech multiples. Real yields are rising alongside nominal yields, a direct headwind for Nasdaq-100's growth-heavy composition. Mega-cap tech (Google, Microsoft, Meta) are signaling massive capex and equity dilution, further spooking investors. Semiconductors — a core NDX pillar — crashed their worst day in 6 years. The USD rallied decisively above 100, headwind-ing tech's international revenue. This is a classic risk-off unwind, with Nasdaq down 4.18% (worst since April 2025) as capital rotates out of momentum growth. The move is in Phase 3 and extended; further downside is possible but chasing shorts here is late-stage.
catalysts_ahead: CPI / Core CPI data (next ~1 week) — any surprise higher resets rate expectations further
PCE inflation print — critical for Fed policy signal
FOMC meeting / Fed communications — Warsh will face immediate pressure on rate trajectory
SpaceX IPO (imminent per news) — test of appetite for mega-cap / growth IPOs in this environment
NVDA / MSFT / GOOGL / AAPL earnings and capex/guidance updates — individual mega-cap moves can swing NDX 2-3%
Apple WWDC (mentioned 2026) — Tim Cook's AI strategy and stock valuation credibility on the line
China-Taiwan tensions and chip export controls — could spike volatility in semiconductor holdings
Weekly jobless claims and economic data — monitor for re-acceleration or deceleration in labor market
screening.risk_warning: Confirmation that inflation is cooling (soft CPI print) or Fed signals dovish pivot/rate cuts would invalidate this bearish bias and flip NAS100 back to bullish. Additionally, any significant pullback (>2-3%) could trigger technical bounce / short covering that retraces 30-50% of this drop in the near term. If mega-cap earnings beat with strong guidance on AI demand, that too could stabilize / reverse sentiment.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended in Phase 3; avoid chasing shorts. Wait for a pullback / retest of 29000–29200 resistance or a day-session stabilization before considering fresh short entries. If shorts are already on, hold through support tests but do not add at lows.

factors.name: NFP Report & Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: May NFP +172K vs 85K expected, prior months revised upward. Treasury yields surged across curve (2Y +12bps, 10Y +6bps) as market now prices Fed rate hike probability. Hot labor data eliminates rate cut hopes and pressures long-duration tech.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Real Rates
factors.summary: 10-year yields spiking on NFP surprise. Rising nominal and real rates compress growth equity multiples, particularly tech with high duration characteristics. This is the primary mechanism driving today's -4.18% NASDAQ rout.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Equity Issuance Risk
factors.summary: Google just completed $85B equity raise; Meta now reportedly considering tens of billions in stock offering to fund AI push (FT). Microsoft also mentioned as potential issuer. Massive dilution risk across hyperscalers pressuring sector sentiment.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Collapse
factors.summary: Chip sector suffers worst day in 6 years per MarketWatch. Marvell, Micron shares tumbled. Korean Won weakness on semiconductor pullback adds pressure. This is core NDX sector undergoing material technical damage.

factors.name: AI CapEx Funding Concerns
factors.summary: Alphabet expects capex up to $190B this year (double 2025), turning to equity markets. Bond investors pleased but equity holders face dilution. Market questioning sustainability of AI buildout economics and margin profiles.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation (Risk-Off)
factors.summary: Bitcoin cracked $60K, lowest since October 2024, down on strong jobs report and higher yields pressuring risk assets. NDX-BTC correlation remains intact in risk-off mode, signaling broad de-risking across speculative tech.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY rallied to near 100.10 from 99.16 intraday on NFP beat. Strengthening dollar headwind for NDX international revenue exposure (meaningful portion of mega-cap sales).

factors.name: Fed Policy Path
factors.summary: Hot jobs report puts Fed cuts 'further out of reach' per news. Citigroup maintains lonely call for 3 cuts but consensus shifting hawkish. Chair Warsh facing pressure; Trump commented he'd like lower rates but will leave to Warsh, citing October meeting.

factors.name: Market Internals & Momentum
factors.summary: NASDAQ down -4.18%, worst day since April 2025. Session lows reached -4.5%. S&P -2.65%, Dow -1.35%. Investors cooling on momentum stocks. Historic weekly run halted with tech selloff leading. Technical damage mounting.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Overhang
factors.summary: Blockbuster SpaceX IPO set to test high-flying rally. Large new supply coming to market. Google deal with SpaceX/xAI for compute ($920M/month) adds complexity. May face long road to S&P 500 inclusion, creating near-term absorption concern.

factors.name: VIX & Volatility
factors.summary: Sharp -4%+ NASDAQ decline indicates volatility spike. VXN (Nasdaq vol) likely elevated. Options positioning likely shifting defensive. Classic risk-off volatility expansion undermines tech premium.

factors.name: Sector Rotation Evidence
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs notes earnings growth critical for sustained gains, but corporate profits as % GDP at record high suggests limited upside. 'Good news is bad news' reflex returning—strong economy makes stocks nervous due to rate implications.

confirmation.narrative: The May NFP blowout (+172K vs 85K expected) triggered a violent tech selloff as Treasury yields surged and Fed rate hike probabilities spiked. The NASDAQ plunged -4.18%, its worst day since April 2025, with semiconductors suffering their worst day in 6 years. The bear case is compounded by mega-cap equity issuance (Google's $85B, Meta considering tens of billions) to fund AI capex, raising dilution fears precisely as rising rates compress growth multiples. The move is Phase 2—well underway with -2.73% already printed in 24h—but momentum remains strongly bearish with multiple structural headwinds converging.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI report (next major inflation data)
FOMC meeting (next decision on rate path)
Apple WWDC (June, Tim Cook's final conference as CEO—AI strategy critical)
SpaceX IPO pricing and allocation
NVDA/MSFT/META earnings (next quarterly reports)
Any Fed speakers responding to NFP data
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if Fed speakers push back aggressively on rate hike expectations, or if yields reverse sharply lower. Treasury yield consolidation or retreat below pre-NFP levels would remove primary pressure on tech multiples. Also watch for policy pivot signals or unexpected dovish commentary from Chair Warsh.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already extended -4.18% intraday; avoid chasing here. Wait for bounce to 29,200-29,400 resistance zone (prior support, now overhead) or Dead Cat Bounce into early next week before re-entering shorts. Alternatively, if sells through 28,500 support, that confirms breakdown for continuation lower.

factors.name: NFP Blowout + Fed Hike Re-pricing
factors.summary: May NFP came in at 172K vs 85K expected per MarketWatch/Bloomberg, crushing soft-landing narrative. Treasury yields spiked across the curve (2Y +12bps, 10Y +6bps), directly compressing long-duration tech multiples. KPMG Chief Economist cited 25bps rate hike expectations now priced for 2026.

factors.name: Real Yields Surge (Proxy: TLT, IEF moves)
factors.summary: Nominal yields rocketing on hawkish Fed repricing. Real yields (TIPS) rising sharply on Friday, the single largest headwind for duration-heavy Nasdaq mega-caps (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, NVDA, TSLA). This directly pressures forward P/E multiples.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Capex Dilution Panic
factors.summary: Google sold record $85B in equity this week; Meta now considering 'tens of billions' in new stock issuance per FT/Bloomberg; Microsoft rumored to tap equity for AI infrastructure per Investinglive. Equity dilution + uncertainty on capex ROI weighing on sentiment.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Collapse
factors.summary: Chip sector posted worst day in 6 years per MarketWatch. Marvell, Micron, NVDA all tanked. Nasdaq-100 heavily indexed to semiconductors; this sector weakness alone cascades to broader index.

factors.name: Extended Move (PHASE 3)
factors.summary: NAS100 down -2.73% in 24h, -1.47% in 4h; Nasdaq Composite fell 4.18% on session (worst day since April 2025 per Investinglive). Move already underway for ~12+ hours; extended decline reduces new-shorts' edge.

factors.name: Your Bias History: Consistent SELL
factors.summary: All three prior reads (17:54, 19:53, 21:26 on 2026-06-05) flagged SELL 7-9/10. This is a sustained, multi-cycle conviction signal, not a whipsaw. Bias held through session.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY +0.94% approx)
factors.summary: USD rallied to 100.10 on hot NFP per FXStreet. Many NAS100 names (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, INTC, AMD) have ~40-50% revenue from overseas; stronger dollar headwind to earnings translation.

factors.name: Risk-Off Cascade (Crypto, Commodities)
factors.summary: Bitcoin crashed below $60K (lowest since Oct 2024), gold fell $143 or 3.2% to $4,333, silver down ~8% daily. Broad risk-off sentiment confirms equities weakness is structural, not local-tech isolated.

factors.name: Insider Selling Acceleration
factors.summary: Multiple Form 144 filings on Friday: WDC CLO ($235K), Krystal Bio CEO ($7.7M), Ichor Holdings CEO ($962K). Pattern of insiders distributing into strength before selloff is a micro-confirm signal.

factors.name: Retail/Momentum Sentiment Cracked
factors.summary: Bloomberg headline: 'Tech Selloff, Bitcoin Drop Test Retail Investor Strength' & 'AI trade is sucking blood out of crypto.' Momentum/AI-chasing retail now forced to capitulate; risk of cascade selling.

key_levels.support: 28500–28300 (prior 4h low zone); if broken, 27800–27500 (daily structural support)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 triggered a violent selloff Friday (down 4.18% composite, NAS100 down ~2.73%) following a hotter-than-expected May NFP (172K vs 85K) that obliterated the soft-landing narrative and sent Treasury yields soaring (10Y +6bps, 2Y +12bps). This real-yield surge is a hammer blow to duration-sensitive mega-cap tech; simultaneously, MSFT, GOOGL, and META are now flagging massive equity dilution for AI capex, compounding multiple compression. The semiconductor sector—core to Nasdaq weight—suffered its worst day in 6 years, and insider selling has accelerated. Your prior three reads all pegged SELL with 7–9/10 conviction; this sustained bias across a multi-hour session is meaningful. The move is in Phase 3 (extended), and while technicals are broken, late shorts face whipsaw risk into oversold conditions.
catalysts_ahead: Monday (2026-06-06, pre-market): Any tech guidance updates, FOMC speculation commentary
Tuesday–Wednesday: CPI/PCE data if not yet released; potential meta-cap earnings (Apple WWDC keynote event)
Next FOMC (date TBD in June/July): Market is pricing 25bps hike—if Chair Warsh signals hawkish tilt, continued pressure; if signals pause/cut, sharp reversal
Mega-cap earnings season ramp: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA capex guidance critical—any disappointment = deeper selloff
Equity dilution updates from META, MSFT, GOOGL on capital raise programs
screening.risk_warning: Primary invalidation: if any major Fed official (Warsh, Barr, Powell surrogate) signals immediate rate-cut path or softer stance, bond yields compress and duration equities bounce hard (2–3% upside in hours). Secondary: if next CPI/PCE surprise lower, risk-off unwinds and flight-to-safety reverses, triggering tech relief rally. Tertiary: if Mag-7 mega-cap earnings crush expectations despite capex, multiple compression bottoms and FOMO re-acceleration lifts NAS100 1–2% within days. Beware oversold bounces into 29200–29400 resistance—do not chase shorts into that zone.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended (Phase 3); wait for retest of 29200–29400 resistance or intraday bounce pullback before adding shorts. If market gaps down Monday, use any 0.5–1% bounce as entry for fresh shorts targeting 28300–28000. For shorts already on board from prior sessions, lock partial profits now and trail stops below support.

factors.name: NFP Blowout & Fed Rate Hike Repricing
factors.summary: May NFP came in at 172K vs 85K expected (Bloomberg), with prior months revised higher. KPMG Chief Economist notes bond market now pricing 25bp hike in 2026. Fed rate hike expectations are kryptonite for long-duration growth stocks.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: Treasury yields rocketed across the curve following NFP—2Y up 12bp, 10Y up 6bp (FXStreet). Rising nominal yields compress valuations on high-multiple tech stocks that dominate NAS100.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Strong jobs data with persistent service-sector inflation (per KPMG's Swonk) pushes real yields higher, directly compressing long-duration tech multiples that comprise 50%+ of NDX.

factors.name: Semiconductor Sector Collapse
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports chip sector suffered worst day in 6 years; Marvell and Micron tumbled despite Marvell's S&P 500 inclusion. Semis are core NDX weight and AI-trade bellwether.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Equity Dilution Risk
factors.summary: Meta considering tens of billions in stock offering (FT/Investing.com), Alphabet seeking fresh capital with capex reaching $190B (double last year). Meta down 6.56% intraday. Dilution fears hitting sentiment across mega-caps.

factors.name: Momentum Reversal & Risk-Off
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports S&P 500 saw $1.8T wipeout, Nasdaq biggest point drop on record. Bitcoin cracked $60K (lowest since Oct 2024), signaling broad risk-off pivot from momentum trades including tech.

factors.name: VIX & Volatility Spike
factors.summary: NASDAQ down 4.18% (worst day since April 2025 per Investing.com), with session lows near -4.5%. Volatility expansion and dealer hedging flows exacerbate selling pressure in high-gamma names.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY rallied from 99.16 to above 100.10 post-NFP (FXStreet). NDX components derive ~40% revenue internationally; dollar strength headwind for earnings and FX translation.

factors.name: Insider Selling Wave
factors.summary: News shows cluster of insider sales: Western Digital CLO $235K, Ichor CEO $962K, Krystal execs $7.7M each. While not NDX-specific, signals caution at leadership level amid valuation concerns.

factors.name: AI Capex Fatigue
factors.summary: Goldman's Bordlethwait notes earnings growth critical to sustain rally; mega-cap capex doubling (Alphabet $190B) without immediate ROI visibility. Market rotating away from 'AI at any cost' narrative.

factors.name: Upcoming Tech Catalysts
factors.summary: Apple WWDC next week (Cook's final developer conference, AI legacy at stake per CNBC) could provide temporary support, but faces high bar after today's selloff. SpaceX IPO and xAI deals are noise for NDX.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 is experiencing a violent repricing as the May NFP blowout (172K vs 85K expected) has flipped Fed expectations from cuts to potential hikes, sending Treasury yields surging and crushing tech valuations. The semiconductor sector posted its worst day in 6 years, mega-caps face equity dilution concerns (Meta, Alphabet raising tens of billions), and broad risk-off is evident with Bitcoin collapsing below $60K. The -4.18% Nasdaq drop marks the largest point decline on record, with move now in Phase 2 but still has room to run given rate repricing incomplete and weekend risk aversion likely.
catalysts_ahead: Apple WWDC (June 9-13) - AI strategy and Siri agentic platform unveil
Fed blackout period begins June 7 ahead of June 18 FOMC decision
Next CPI print (June 11) will confirm/refute persistent inflation narrative
Nvidia GTC follow-up and semiconductor supply chain updates
Meta/Alphabet equity offering details and pricing
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if Fed officials push back hard on rate hike speculation in next 48 hours, or if Apple WWDC delivers transformative AI narrative that reignites momentum trade. Sustained break back above 29,400 with declining yields would signal reversal.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2 (underway but not exhausted); best entry is short on any relief rally to 29,200-29,400 resistance zone, or add to shorts below 28,500 if support breaks on Monday with continued yield pressure.
